one_sentence_reason: Older patients may have less physiological reserve and showed the strongest simple association with ESI.
Use of an oxygen device suggests respiratory support needs and was strongly associated with higher acuity.
Chest pain is a high-risk ED presentation because it may indicate acute coronary syndrome or other cardiac emergencies.
Low oxygen saturation directly reflects respiratory compromise.
Shortness of breath may indicate respiratory or cardiac deterioration.
Altered mental status can reflect neurologic, metabolic, septic, or toxicologic emergencies.
Extremely low or high glucose can require urgent intervention.
Systolic blood pressure helps flag shock, bleeding, dehydration, or severe cardiovascular stress.
Heart rate reflects pain, fever, anxiety, shock, arrhythmia, or physiologic stress.
Respiratory rate is an early warning sign for clinical deterioration.
